req.title: Export employee directory — variant 5
req.statement: An hr-admin shall export the employee directory as CSV with PII redacted for employees who opted out of public directory. Additionally, the action requires two-factor authentication confirmation.

doc.reasoning: The violation was that test[2] had kind=negative but heuristic=STC. The STC (State Transition Coverage) heuristic is a structural/design technique that is not inherently negative; the test exercises the 2FA state machine where the transition to export-authorised is blocked by missing OTP — this is correctly a negative scenario, but STC is the wrong heuristic label for a negative kind. The fix is to change the heuristic for test[2] from STC to NEG, which correctly classifies it as a negative/error-pathway test (role or precondition violation). The 2FA state-machine rationale is preserved in the trace and description. All other tests are preserved exactly as they passed validation.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_export_csv_pii_redacted_for_optout_employees
preconditions: At least one employee has opted out of public directory
At least one employee has not opted out
actions: GET /api/employees/export.csv with Authorization and X-OTP-Token headers
expected: Opted-out employee rows have PII fields replaced with REDACTED
Non-opted-out employee rows contain real PII
trace: R-WEB-086: hr-admin export triggers CSV stream
R-WEB-086: PII redacted for opted-out employees
tests.executable:
import pytest
import csv, io

def test_hr_admin_export_csv_pii_redacted_for_optout_employees(base_url, get_hr_admin_token, complete_2fa, opted_out_employee_id, non_opted_out_employee_id):
    token = get_hr_admin_token()
    otp = complete_2fa(token)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "X-OTP-Token": otp}
    resp = <http_client>.get(f"{base_url}/api/employees/export.csv", headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert "text/csv" in resp.headers.get("Content-Type", "")
    assert "attachment" in resp.headers.get("Content-Disposition", "")
    reader = csv.DictReader(io.StringIO(resp.text))
    rows = {r["employee_id"]: r for r in reader}
    opted_out_row = rows[str(opted_out_employee_id)]
    non_opted_row = rows[str(non_opted_out_employee_id)]
    for pii_field in ("email", "phone", "address"):
        assert opted_out_row[pii_field] == "REDACTED", f"{pii_field} must be redacted"
        assert non_opted_row[pii_field] != "REDACTED", f"{pii_field} must be visible"

tests.name: test_non_admin_role_receives_403_on_export
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as a standard employee (role: employee, not hr-admin)
2FA token is technically valid but irrelevant
expected: Response body contains an error message indicating insufficient privileges
trace: R-WEB-086: error pathway — 403 if not hr-admin
tests.executable:
def test_non_admin_role_receives_403_on_export(base_url, get_employee_token):
    token = get_employee_token()
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
    resp = <http_client>.get(f"{base_url}/api/employees/export.csv", headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body
    error_text = body.get("error", body.get("message", "")).lower()
    assert any(kw in error_text for kw in ("forbidden", "unauthorized", "privilege", "role"))

tests.name: test_export_blocked_when_2fa_not_confirmed
preconditions: 2FA step has NOT been completed (no OTP token presented)
expected: Response body indicates 2FA confirmation is required
trace: R-WEB-086: 2FA confirmation required — absent OTP must be rejected
R-WEB-086: NEG — precondition violation: export-authorised state requires completed 2FA step
tests.executable:
def test_export_blocked_when_2fa_not_confirmed(base_url, get_hr_admin_token):
    token = get_hr_admin_token()
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
    # Deliberately omit X-OTP-Token
    resp = <http_client>.get(f"{base_url}/api/employees/export.csv", headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code in (401, 403)
    body = resp.json()
    combined = str(body).lower()
    assert any(kw in combined for kw in ("2fa", "otp", "two-factor", "mfa", "verification"))

tests.name: test_rate_limit_429_on_excessive_export_requests
preconditions: Rate-limit window is fresh (counter at zero)
Rate limit for this endpoint is known (injected via rate_limit_threshold fixture)
actions: Obtain hr-admin session token and OTP via <get_hr_admin_token> and <complete_2fa>
Send exactly rate_limit_threshold requests — all should succeed
expected: First N requests (up to threshold) return HTTP 200
trace: R-WEB-086: BVA boundary at rate-limit threshold N and N+1
tests.executable:
def test_rate_limit_429_on_excessive_export_requests(base_url, get_hr_admin_token, complete_2fa, rate_limit_threshold, reset_rate_limit):
    reset_rate_limit("export_csv")
    token = get_hr_admin_token()
    otp = complete_2fa(token)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "X-OTP-Token": otp}
    url = f"{base_url}/api/employees/export.csv"
    for i in range(rate_limit_threshold):
        r = <http_client>.get(url, headers=headers)
        assert r.status_code == 200, f"Request {i+1} expected 200, got {r.status_code}"
    over_limit = <http_client>.get(url, headers=headers)
    assert over_limit.status_code == 429
    assert "Retry-After" in over_limit.headers

tests.name: test_optout_decision_table_mixed_employee_set
preconditions: Caller is authenticated as hr-admin with valid 2FA OTP
actions: Parse the CSV and locate rows for employees A, B, C
expected: Employee A row: PII fields are REDACTED (opted out, active)
Employee B row: PII fields are real values (not opted out)
Employee C row: PII fields are REDACTED (opted out, regardless of active status)
trace: R-WEB-086: PII redacted per opt-out — decision table covering opted_out x active combinations
tests.executable:
def test_optout_decision_table_mixed_employee_set(base_url, get_hr_admin_token, complete_2fa, employee_ids_by_scenario):
    token = get_hr_admin_token()
    otp = complete_2fa(token)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "X-OTP-Token": otp}
    resp = <http_client>.get(f"{base_url}/api/employees/export.csv", headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    import csv, io
    rows = {r["employee_id"]: r for r in csv.DictReader(io.StringIO(resp.text))}
    id_a = str(employee_ids_by_scenario["opted_out_active"])
    id_b = str(employee_ids_by_scenario["not_opted_out_active"])
    id_c = str(employee_ids_by_scenario["opted_out_inactive"])
    for pii in ("email", "phone", "address"):
        assert rows[id_a][pii] == "REDACTED", f"Employee A {pii} must be redacted"
        assert rows[id_b][pii] != "REDACTED", f"Employee B {pii} must not be redacted"
        assert rows[id_c][pii] == "REDACTED", f"Employee C {pii} must be redacted"
